title: Shift Manager Runbook
purpose: Enter live contest sales and returned Bingo cards, verify the visible boards, reconcile receipts, and hand off exceptions without changing contest rules or inventing check data.

allowed: Inspect the active contest, prize, goals, servers, boards, and recent operation receipts
Record positive quantities for active contest item-count targets
Log a returned current Bingo card
Record a complete check only when every source fact is known
Correct a source check with an audit reason
Write evidence and discovery artifacts inside the assigned run directory
forbidden: Change prizes, goals, scoring, or game rules
Lock a race, award a goal board, or draw a prize winner
Use a negative contest tally as an offset
Invent full-check facts to represent a contest-only quantity
Edit product source, seed data, specs, or prompts during an operating run
Use SQL or an API to conceal a blocked UI discovery step

steps.title: Confirm the active shift contest
steps.instruction: Open Dashboard and state the active contest, prize, goals, and remaining time before entering sales.
preconditions: SIM is running against the assigned disposable database
expected: The operator can name the active contest and prize
The Dashboard and operations snapshot report the same contest ID
forbidden_shortcuts: Assuming the contest from a previous shift
Reading the database directly instead of checking the UI
observations: Could a shift manager immediately tell what item to sell and what can be won?
Is the time window unambiguous?
recovery: Stop if the UI and snapshot identify different contests

steps.title: Open a server's contest tally
steps.instruction: Open Sales Data, find the scenario server, and select Add contest sales so the tool unfolds beneath that server row.
expected: The tally tool opens inline under the selected server
Only active contest item-count targets are offered
forbidden_shortcuts: Searching the full menu to find a contest item
Inventing a server or item ID
observations: Was the correct server easy to find?
Did the row stay visually connected to its open tool?
recovery: Stop if the target item is absent or an unrelated menu item is offered

steps.title: Review the contest quantity
steps.instruction: Choose the scenario contest item when necessary, enter the positive quantity, and review the automatically calculated count and value before submitting.
preconditions: The intended server tally is open
The active contest and expected contest ID have not changed
expected: No party size or manual subtotal is required
The item value is calculated automatically
The quantity is a positive whole number
observations: Did the form ask for anything the shift manager should not need to know?
Was the automatic calculation trustworthy and readable?
recovery: Do not submit if the server, item, or calculation is wrong

steps.title: Submit and reconcile contest sales
steps.instruction: Submit the tally once, retain its operation receipt, and verify that only the scoped item count changed.
preconditions: A unique operation ID is assigned
expected: One append-only contest sales event is recorded
The server's active item count increases by the entered quantity
Check count, sales, and tip metrics do not change
forbidden_shortcuts: Retrying with a new operation ID before reconciliation
Treating a full check as the tally
observations: Did the success message identify server, item, quantity, and resulting total?
Could a retry be mistaken for a second sale?
recovery: Read the original operation receipt before retrying

steps.title: Verify the live gameboard
steps.instruction: Open the relevant Sales Gameboard and verify the server score reflects the successful tally.
preconditions: The contest tally receipt is successful
expected: The visible board agrees with the read-back snapshot
The correct server moved by the expected amount
forbidden_shortcuts: Using the API snapshot as a substitute for viewing the board
observations: How long did it take to connect the entry to the correct board?
Was it clear whether the board updated live or needed refresh?
recovery: Record a data mismatch and stop additional entries if the receipt and board disagree

steps.title: Mark a returned Bingo card
steps.instruction: Select the returned current card and mark only completed squares. Click individual squares or drag along one logical row, column, or diagonal; ignore near-miss squares that do not continue that line.
preconditions: The physical or scenario card belongs to the selected server
The card has not been re-randomized since distribution
expected: Marked cells match the returned card
FREE counts automatically
A drag gesture does not collect unrelated near-miss squares
forbidden_shortcuts: Adding off-line squares from an imprecise drag
observations: Did click-and-drag follow the operator's intended line?
Could the manager spot a stale or wrong server card before submission?
recovery: Stop if the current UI card differs from the returned card

steps.title: Log the returned Bingo card
steps.instruction: Select Log turned-in card once, retain the operation receipt, and read the line, daily-win, and wheel-entry result.
preconditions: The marked card has been visually checked
A unique operation ID is assigned
expected: The submission reports completed lines
At most one daily win is awarded for the server and business date
Wheel entries are added only while the drawing is open
forbidden_shortcuts: Submitting a second time with a new operation ID
Assuming a daily win from line count alone
observations: Did the result distinguish completed lines from daily-win eligibility?
Was a duplicate submission understandable?
recovery: Reconcile the original receipt after interruption
Stop on stale-card or stale-contest errors

steps.title: Record a complete check when required
steps.instruction: Use Add full check only when the scenario provides the server, opened time, party size, every item line, and complete check facts. Let item lines calculate subtotal automatically.
preconditions: The scenario explicitly requires a complete check
expected: The check reconciles without duplicate subtotal entry
Check-based metrics update from the saved source record
forbidden_shortcuts: Inventing party size, items, tax, tip, or totals
Using a full check for contest-only sales
observations: Did the workflow require duplicated arithmetic?
Was the difference between contest sales and full checks clear?
recovery: Skip and record the missing source facts rather than fabricate them

steps.title: Correct a source check with a reason
steps.instruction: When the scenario identifies an incorrect source check, expand Checks beneath the server, edit that check inline, and give a specific correction reason.
preconditions: The incorrect check and corrected source facts are known
expected: The correction remains associated with the source check
The audit trail preserves the reason and prior values
Derived metrics reflect the corrected check
observations: Could the source check be located without leaving the server context?
Was the audit impact clear before saving?
recovery: Stop if the original check cannot be uniquely identified

steps.title: Document entry exceptions
steps.instruction: If a contest tally was entered incorrectly or any live workflow cannot recover safely, record the exact server, item, quantity, time, operation ID, observed result, and needed recovery for manager handoff.
expected: No negative offset is created
The exception is precise enough for a manager to investigate
The observation identifies a missing capability when appropriate
forbidden_shortcuts: Changing source code during the run
observations: Could the shift manager correct the mistake safely?
What information did the handoff require SIM to remember externally?
recovery: Leave the successful append-only event intact and escalate the referenced receipt

steps.title: Reconcile and hand off the shift
steps.instruction: Reconcile every intended action to one operation receipt, verify required evidence, record friction even for passing steps, and complete the shift summary without fixing the product.
preconditions: The scenario ended or reached a documented blocker
expected: Every intended write is applied, already applied, blocked, or explicitly skipped
All anomalies and evidence gaps are handed off
Candidate product needs remain unaccepted pending human triage
observations: What did SIM force the shift manager to remember outside the app?
Which live actions took too many steps?
Which success states were ambiguous?
recovery: Mark missing evidence or unresolved actions explicitly rather than inventing completion
